summary: Summary: Speaker1 (Joe) opens the conversation by recounting an initial disappointment about a college program with a tone of sadness, then quickly reveals acceptance to U.S.C. (and that his girlfriend Natalie also got into a school in L.A.), shifting to happiness and feeling excited. Speaker2 (Isabella) responds with surprise and excitement—asking which program, expressing surprise at its ranking, and offering congratulations. The two move from shock to celebration planning, with Speaker2 suggesting partying and getting drunk in an excited and happy mood. They then bond over college choices and shared ambitions in film, with Speaker2 showing frustration about Boston and expressing happiness and excitement about L.A.’s culture. Speaker1 describes dorm life playfully, conveying happiness and neutral moments while denying a teasing label, and Speaker2 stays playful and lighthearted (happiness, excited). The exchange follows a trajectory from brief sadness to sustained surprise, happiness, and excitement, with occasional neutral and frustrated remarks.
